title: palmistry frd.docx
docSource: docx file uploaded by the user.
pageContent:
Below is a detailed Functional Requirements Document (FRD) for your app concept: an LLM‑powered astrology + palmistry guidance app that preserves user identity and context, supports subscriptions, and can work globally in many languages.

You can treat this as a starting spec for product, design, and engineering.



1. Overview

1.1 Product Name (Working)

Product: Whispering Palms – AITagline: Ancient Indian wisdom, modern AI guidance.

1.2 Purpose

Build a mobile/web app where users can:

Upload palm images 

Enter birth details (date, time, place) 

Ask questions (one‑off and via subscriptions) 

Receive automated, LLM‑generated responses styled as “Aarav Dev – The Whispering Sage” 

Have their identity and history tracked so that answers always use the right context. 

1.3 Scope (MVP)

In scope:

User auth & identity management 

Profile & palm/birth‑data storage 

Question submission & answer delivery (chat-style and/or email) 

Subscription plans (1 question/day, 3 questions/day) + one‑off “Deep Outlook” 

LLM orchestration with: 

persona (“Aarav Dev”) 

contextual memory per user 

multilingual input support 

Admin console for monitoring, content review, and user management. 

Out of scope (for MVP):

Real human guru involvement 

Advanced CV/palm line automatic detection (nice-to-have, can be v2) 

Native offline functionality. 



2. User Personas

Seeker in Transition (Primary)

Age 22–45, global, English or non‑English speaker. 

Wants emotional/spiritual guidance on relationships & career. 

Uses mobile, comfortable with subscriptions. 

Curious Skeptic

Tries app for fun but may convert if impressed. 

Low patience; needs fast, clear onboarding. 

Returning Devotee

Has done astrology/palmistry before. 

Wants ongoing, “mentor in my pocket” subscription. 

Admin / Operator

You/your team managing content, reviewing flagged answers, handling refunds, etc. 



3. High‑Level Features

User registration/login (email + password; social login optional later). 

Global onboarding in multiple languages (at least English + auto‑translate for others). 

Profile with: 

Name, gender (optional), country, preferred language. 

Birth date, time, place (with timezone). 

Stored palm images. 

One‑time “Deep Outlook” reading flow. 

Subscription plans: 

Plan A: 1 question/day. 

Plan B: 3 questions/day. 

Chat‑style interface for questions & answers. 

LLM engine with: 

Persona: “Aarav Dev – The Whispering Sage”. 

Palm & chart context retrieval. 

User history and subscription state awareness. 

Admin dashboard (web) for: 

User search & profiles. 

Question/answer audit, moderation tools. 

Subscription & payment monitoring. 

Basic analytics (MAU, questions/day, retention, conversion). 



4. Detailed Functional Requirements

4.1 Platforms

Web app (responsive, PWA if possible). 

Mobile app (Phase 2): iOS/Android using a shared codebase (e.g., React Native/Flutter). 

MVP FRD assumes a responsive web app, mobile web optimized.



4.2 Authentication & Identity Management

Requirements:

Sign‑up

Users can register via: 

Email + password (mandatory). 

Must confirm email (optional for v1; recommended for fraud control). 

On successful registration, create a User ID (UUID). 

Login

Email + password. 

“Forgot password” flow with email reset link. 

Identity & Context

Each request to LLM is associated with: 

user_id 

language_preference 

profile (dob, tob, pob) 

palm_images metadata 

subscription_state 

recent_questions/answers (for context). 

Multi‑device

User can log in from multiple devices; context stored server‑side. 



4.3 User Profile & Onboarding

Onboarding Steps:

Welcome Screen

Explanation of what app does. 

Legal & spiritual disclaimer. 

Basic Details

Name (or alias). 

Country. 

Preferred interface language (default: English). 

Birth Details

Date of birth (DD/MM/YYYY or localized). 

Exact time of birth (HH:MM, and “approximate” checkbox if unknown). 

Place of birth (auto‑complete + timezone detection). 

Palm Images Upload

Right palm (front). 

Left palm (front). 

Optional: side views or fingers close‑up. 

Image validation (min resolution, file type). 

Consent

Explicit consent for: 

Storing images and birth data. 

Using LLM to generate responses. 

Non‑medical, non‑financial, non‑legal advice disclaimer. 

Profile must be editable later (except birth data optionally locked or change‑tracked).



4.4 Question & Answer Flows

4.4.1 One‑Time “Deep Outlook”

User Flow:

Choose “Deep Outlook Reading”. 

Confirm birth & palm data; upload/update if needed. 

Enter up to 3 main questions (free‑text, any language). 

Payment screen → complete payment. 

Post‑payment: 

Create a “Reading Job” with status pending. 

LLM backend generates a structured, long‑form answer: 

Personality & temperament. 

Current cycles/timing. 

Answers to the 3 questions. 

Output stored in DB and shown as a document/chat thread. 

User notified by email/push when ready. 

Functional Requirements:

Each Deep Outlook has: 

reading_id, user_id, created_at, status, input_questions, generated_content. 

Option for user to download as PDF (later). 



4.4.2 Subscription Q&A (Spark/Flame)

Plans:

Spark: 1 question/day. 

Flame: 3 questions/day. 

Rules:

“Per day” based on user’s timezone. 

Unused daily questions do not carry over (clear rule in UI). 

A “question” = one message from user; follow‑up clarifications by LLM may not count as extra “question”. 

User Flow:

User subscribes to Spark or Flame (via in‑app purchase/Stripe/etc.). 

On chat screen: 

Show remaining questions for today: e.g., “You have 1 of 1 questions left today”. 

User types question (any language); optional category tags (Love, Career, Family, Spiritual, Money). 

System checks: 

active_subscription == true 

remaining_daily_questions > 0 

If allowed: 

Create question record, decrement counter. 

Call LLM with full context (see 4.5). 

Display answer in chat. 

Edge Cases:

If quotas exhausted: 

Show friendly message: “You’ve used all your questions for today. New questions open in X hours.” 

If subscription expired: 

Upsell renewal. 



4.5 LLM Orchestration & Context Management

Goals:

Maintain personalized guidance over time. 

Ensure LLM knows: 

Who the user is. 

Their past issues. 

Their past answers. 

Subscription type and expectations. 

Components:

User Context Store

Profile (birth data, country, language). 

Palm image metadata (no need to send images every time; send summary tags). 

Past key insights (optionally distilled from Deep Outlook). 

Last N questions/answers (e.g., last 20). 

Prompt Template

System prompt sets: 

Persona: “You are Aarav Dev – The Whispering Sage, an Indian guru combining Vedic astrology and palmistry…” 

Tone: calm, compassionate, mystical but practical. 

Safety rules: no medical/financial/legal claims; use disclaimers. 

Use of context: “Use birth chart summary and palm traits given below…” 

Message Payload

For each LLM call: 

System prompt (persona + rules). 

Developer message (context: user profile, reading summary). 

User message (question, original language). 

If question not in English: 

Either auto-translate to English before LLM. 

Or instruct LLM to respond in the user’s interface language. 

Context Retrieval Logic

For each new question: 

Load user’s latest Deep Outlook summary (if exists). 

Load user’s last N Q&A pairs. 

Include relevant ones in prompt (or RAG style). 

Palm & Astrology Data

Option A (MVP): Precompute a textual “profile” from user’s birth data & palm images using a fixed pipeline/LLM prompt once. Store as astropalm_profile_text. 

At answer time, pass this astropalm_profile_text instead of images/raw data to reduce cost & complexity. 



4.6 Multilingual Support

Requirements:

Input Language

User can type in any language. 

App detects language (auto or via user setting). 

Translation Strategy

Option A: Use external translation API to translate all input to English → LLM → translate output back. 

Option B: Ask LLM to “understand user language X and respond in that language”. 

UI Language

Initially support: 

English as primary. 

MVP: allow non‑English questions but UI can remain English; later full localization. 

Stored Data

Store original user message + internal English translation (for analytics and future training). 



4.7 Subscription & Billing

Requirements:

Plans

Spark: 1 question/day. 

Flame: 3 questions/day. 

Optionally: Deep Outlook as a one‑time purchasable product. 

Billing

Use payment gateway (e.g., Stripe, in-app purchases later). 

All prices denominated in USD; show currency note in UI. 

Subscription State Machine

trial (optional), 

active, 

past_due, 

canceled, 

expired. 

Daily Quota Reset

Cron job or scheduled task per user’s timezone to reset remaining_questions each day. 

Receipts & Invoices

Email confirmation after purchase or renewal. 



4.8 Notifications

Channels:

Email (MVP). 

Push notifications (for mobile later). 

Events:

Deep Outlook completed. 

Subscription activated, renewed, or expired. 

Daily “You have questions available today” reminder (optional). 



4.9 Admin Console

Features:

User Management

Search users by email/name/id. 

View profile, subscription status, last login date. 

Manually adjust subscription (extend/cancel). 

Conversation View

Read-only view of user questions & generated answers. 

Mark answers as: 

“Reviewed” 

“Flagged” 

Optionally regenerate answer with different prompt. 

Content Flags

Auto‑flag if: 

User mentions self‑harm, violence, illegal activities. 

Model makes certain risky recommendations (detected via heuristics). 

Show flagged items in admin queue. 

Analytics

Total users, active users. 

Active subscriptions by plan. 

Average questions per day. 

LLM cost reports (optional). 



5. Data Model (High-Level)

You can refine later; indicative only:

User

id, email, password_hash, name, country, preferred_language, created_at, timezone. 

UserProfileDetails

user_id, dob, tob, pob, birth_timezone, astropalm_profile_text. 

PalmImage

id, user_id, type (right_front, left_front, etc.), url, uploaded_at. 

Subscription

id, user_id, plan (spark|flame), status, start_date, end_date, next_billing_date, provider_subscription_id. 

DailyQuota

user_id, date, plan, max_questions, remaining_questions. 

Question

id, user_id, subscription_id (nullable for one‑time), source (deep_outlook|subscription), text_original, text_internal_en, created_at. 

Answer

id, question_id, user_id, text, created_at, safety_flags. 

ReadingJob (Deep Outlook)

id, user_id, status, questions, generated_content, created_at, completed_at. 



6. Non‑Functional Requirements

Performance

95% of non‑LLM API calls < 500ms. 

LLM responses typically within 5–20 seconds. 

Availability

99% uptime target for core services. 

Security

HTTPS everywhere. 

Encrypted storage for sensitive data. 

Role‑based access control in admin console. 

Privacy & Compliance

Clear privacy policy and ToS. 

Data deletion on user request (GDPR‑friendly). 

Explicitly position as entertainment / spiritual guidance, not professional advice. 



7. Key User Flows (Summarized)

New user → onboarding → Deep Outlook purchase → receives reading. 

Existing user → subscribes to Spark/Flame → daily questions in chat. 

User runs out of daily quota → sees “quota used” message → upgraded plan suggestion. 

Admin logs in → reviews flagged conversations → updates subscription or blocks user if abuse. 



If you’d like, next I can:

Turn this FRD into a feature breakdown with phases (MVP, v2, v3), or 

Propose a high‑level architecture diagram description (services, databases, LLM gateway, translation layer) that your engineers can implement.
